docs_evidence.claim: Documents the smallest end-to-end Runner plus Mocker HTTP smoke test.
Documents StaticResponseProcessor and the /hello HTTP stub shape.
Schema evidence for Runner YAML top-level sections and HttpStatus fields.
Schema evidence for Mocker YAML top-level sections and StaticResponseProcessor fields.

intent_questions.question: What behavior must be proven?
intent_questions.self_answer: An HTTP Runner transaction must call a Mocker /hello endpoint and the HttpStatus assertion must observe HTTP 200.
intent_questions.risk_if_wrong: The seed would prove only process startup instead of QaaS act/assert behavior.
intent_questions.how_to_override: Provide a different public docs-backed behavior and update the seed YAML plus manifest evidence.

intent_questions.question: What is the system boundary: Runner target, Mocker dependency, hook host, or configuration-as-code?
intent_questions.self_answer: The boundary is a Runner host plus a local Mocker dependency; no top-repo code and no private QaaS source are required.
intent_questions.risk_if_wrong: A broader boundary would need repository-specific contracts and more dependency gates.
intent_questions.how_to_override: Add explicit repo/component contracts before broadening the seed.

intent_questions.question: What public docs/schema path proves the capability exists?
intent_questions.self_answer: The hello-world HTTP guide, StaticResponseProcessor docs, HttpStatus docs, and runner/mocker schema assets prove the YAML shape.
intent_questions.risk_if_wrong: Unsupported YAML fields could be generated for weak models.
intent_questions.how_to_override: Update docs evidence and rerun template validation.

intent_questions.question: What inputs, outputs, and side effects prove success?
intent_questions.self_answer: Input is the Runner HTTP GET; output is a saved transaction with HTTP status 200; side effects are local session data and Allure results.
intent_questions.risk_if_wrong: The assertion could pass against the wrong output name or stale session data.
intent_questions.how_to_override: Change OutputNames and clear storage through the live validation command.

intent_questions.question: Which negative, malformed, outage, retry, cleanup, and observability cases matter?
intent_questions.self_answer: The seed covers only the positive HTTP 200 path; outage and wrong-status cases remain future negative coverage.
intent_questions.risk_if_wrong: The seed may be overinterpreted as broad HTTP coverage.
intent_questions.how_to_override: Add negative seed cases after the positive lifecycle is promotable.

intent_questions.question: Which dependencies must exist: broker, HTTP endpoint, database, Redis, S3, Kubernetes, filesystem, credentials, ports?
intent_questions.self_answer: Only .NET SDK, local QaaS NuGet packages, and an available localhost HTTP port are required.
intent_questions.risk_if_wrong: Port conflicts or missing local packages will block template/build/live evidence.
intent_questions.how_to_override: Pass a different PreferredPort or restore package source, then regenerate the seed.

intent_questions.question: What can run now, and what must be deferred?
intent_questions.self_answer: Template, build, and local live Runner/Mocker validation can run now; executable promotion is deferred until live airgapped weak validation passes.
intent_questions.risk_if_wrong: Dry-run weak validation could be mistaken for live weak-model behavior.
intent_questions.how_to_override: Rerun with AttemptLiveAirgapped when hosted weak-model quota is available.

intent_assumptions.assumption: The local dotnet templates represent the public QaaS Runner and Mocker host pattern.
intent_assumptions.why_safe: The public docs invoke dotnet new qaas-runner and qaas-mocker for these hosts.
intent_assumptions.risk_if_wrong: Generated hosts may not match published package behavior.
intent_assumptions.how_to_override: Use an explicitly provided public host template or package version.

intent_assumptions.assumption: A localhost port can be allocated for the Mocker HTTP server.
intent_assumptions.why_safe: The generator probes for an available port before writing YAML.
intent_assumptions.risk_if_wrong: A race could still occupy the port before live validation.

intent_assumptions.assumption: The positive HTTP 200 seed is a lifecycle seed, not full HTTP coverage.
intent_assumptions.why_safe: The manifest stays blocked and records missing negative coverage.
intent_assumptions.how_to_override: Add separate negative cases and assertions.

intent_assumptions.assumption: StaticResponseProcessor is available through the local QaaS.Common.Processors package.
intent_assumptions.why_safe: The local package source contains QaaS.Common.Processors and the docs document StaticResponseProcessor.
intent_assumptions.risk_if_wrong: Mocker template validation or live startup will fail.
intent_assumptions.how_to_override: Provide a package source with the documented processor package.

intent_assumptions.assumption: HttpStatus uses StatusCode and OutputNames despite helloWorldHttp prose showing ExpectedStatus.
intent_assumptions.why_safe: The schema and HttpStatus reference page agree on StatusCode and OutputNames.
intent_assumptions.risk_if_wrong: The Runner template command would reject the assertion configuration.
intent_assumptions.how_to_override: Regenerate from updated schema if the public schema changes.

intent_assumptions.assumption: Airgapped dry-run output is useful prompt assembly evidence only.
intent_assumptions.why_safe: The weak-model gate forbids accepting dry-run weak validation as behavior.
intent_assumptions.risk_if_wrong: The seed could be promoted without a weak model actually following the skill.
intent_assumptions.how_to_override: Rerun live airgapped validation and require status passed.

intent_assumptions.assumption: No top-repo contract is needed for this docs-owned seed.
intent_assumptions.why_safe: The seed's source_repository is qaas-docs rather than a target repo.
intent_assumptions.risk_if_wrong: The seed would not prove top-250 repo readiness.
intent_assumptions.how_to_override: Keep top-repo promotion separate and require repo contracts.

cases.scenario: Runner calls the Mocker /hello endpoint and asserts HTTP 200.
setup: Start the generated Mocker host on localhost port 18080.
action: Run dotnet run --project runner -- run test.qaas.yaml -e --no-env.
cleanup: Clear generated session-data and allure-results on the next regeneration.
cases.blocked_reason: Template/build/local live validation is recorded, but live airgapped weak-model validation has not passed.

setup: Build the generated Mocker host with QaaS.Common.Processors.
action: Run dotnet run --project mocker -- run mocker.qaas.yaml --no-env.
assertions: Readiness probe receives HTTP 200 from /hello before Runner starts.
cleanup: Stop the Mocker process after Runner validation.
cases.blocked_reason: Template/build/local live validation is recorded, but live airgapped weak-model validation has not passed.

cleanup: Regeneration removes prior promotion seed directory after path guard validation

required_evidence: Public API, CLI, or runtime contract
C# build result when code artifacts exist
Live QaaS run/act/assert result when dependency gates are ready

validation_sequence: Generate Runner and Mocker hosts with dotnet new.
Pin local QaaS packages from D:\QaaS\_localfeed\packages.
Write schema-backed Runner and Mocker YAML from public docs.
Run dotnet build for both generated hosts.
Run dotnet run -- template for both YAML files.
Start Mocker, wait for /hello, and run Runner with -e.
Run airgapped weak-model validation; dry-run evidence is not promotion evidence.

required_evidence: Live weak-model transcript for this seed with weak_validation_passed: true and dry_run: false.
source_only_blockers.unblock_instruction: Rerun generate-promotion-seed.ps1 with -AttemptLiveAirgapped after hosted weak-model quota is available.
